en: It's versatile. Good. Thank you very much.
Inception. Now, before you bother telling me it's impossible...
No, it's perfectly possible, it's just bloody difficult.
Just difficult. It's Arthur who keeps telling me it can't be done.
Hmm, Arthur. Are you still working on that stick in the mud?
ko: 아서. 아직도 그 고집쟁이랑 일하고 있어?

en: He is good at what he does, right? Oh, he's the best. He has no imagination.
Not like you. Listen, if you're going to perform Inception, you need imagination.
Let me ask you something. Have you done it before?
We tried it. We got the idea in place, but it didn't take.
You didn't plant it deep enough? No, it's not just about depth.
You need the simplest version of the idea in order for it to grow naturally in your subject's mind.
ko: 아이디어의 가장 단순한 버전이 필요해, 그래야 자연스럽게 대상의 마음에 자랄 수 있어.

en: It's a very subtle art. So, what is this idea that you need to plant?
We need the heir of a major corporation to dissolve his father's empire.
ko: 우리는 대기업의 상속자가 아버지의 제국을 해체해야 해.

en: You see right there, you have various political motivations and anti-monopolistic sentiments
ko: 바로 그 점에서 다양한 정치적 동기와 반독점 감정 등이 있어.

en: and so forth, but all of that stuff, that's all. I'm not going to tell you.
It's really at the mercy of your subject's prejudice, you see.
ko: 정말로 대상의 편견에 좌우되는 거야, 알겠지.
